tiers.label: High Ground
tiers.description: AI amplifies your value. These roles benefit directly from AI handling the low-value work, freeing you to do more of what only a human can do.
tiers.for_current_workers: Deepen your expertise — AI will amplify you
tiers.for_career_explorers: Strong entry — commit fully and specialize

tiers.label: Solid Ground
tiers.description: Well-positioned with real protection. These roles have meaningful defenses against automation, though the ceiling isn't unlimited.
tiers.for_current_workers: Stay current, keep building your edge
tiers.for_career_explorers: Good choice — level up your skills before you enter

tiers.label: Shifting Ground
tiers.description: One strong factor, one real threat. These roles have protection in some areas but genuine vulnerability in others — the outcome depends on how you position yourself.
tiers.for_current_workers: Cross-train now — add adjacent skills before you need them
tiers.for_career_explorers: Enter with eyes open — build adjacent skills alongside this role

tiers.label: Thin Ice
tiers.description: Automation is already underway. These roles are losing ground to AI and the window to act is narrowing.
tiers.for_current_workers: Start your pivot while you still have runway
tiers.for_career_explorers: Look elsewhere unless you have a specific reason to enter

tiers.label: Fault Line
tiers.description: These roles may not exist in their current form within a decade. Displacement is already happening in many cases.
tiers.for_current_workers: Time to move — transition on your terms before circumstances force it
tiers.for_career_explorers: Skip it — redirect that training investment elsewhere

role_resilience_score.description: Measures how well the role itself resists AI automation across 10 attributes. Scores the job, not the labor market around it.

projected_growth.description: BLS 10-year projected employment growth rate for this occupation.

projected_job_openings.description: BLS annual projected job openings — reflects both new jobs and replacement demand.

final_ranking.description: Composite score combining role resilience (50%), projected growth (30%), and job openings (20%). Used to place each occupation into a tier.
